summary: Well-drilled tournament navigators under Pape Thiaw (in charge since December 2024) — they won 1-0 in the quarter, semi and final of the 2025 AFCON, only to have the title controversially stripped in March (players walked off in protest at a Morocco penalty). A high-pressing, control-oriented 4-3-3 seeking redemption.
style: High-pressing 4-3-3 that defends on the front foot, with midfielders jumping quickly onto central passes from a mid-block. Sadio Mané drifts inside from the left (Ndiaye/Mbaye mirror on the right), attacking full-backs provide width, and Idrissa Gueye drops between the centre-backs. Wants control and threatens with early balls into the half-spaces.

counterReliance.note: Front-foot press and control; real counter threat off regains

strengths: Possession control — 2nd at AFCON (61.1%) and 2nd for big chances created
High press and quick midfield jumps onto central passes
Early balls into the half-spaces to release runners (twice vs England in a 3-1 win)
Tournament know-how and a proven comeback streak
weaknesses: Tendency to overplay in their own half — high-pressing teams can force turnovers
An ageing core (Mané, Koulibaly, Mendy, Gueye all mid-30s) likely at their final World Cup

keyPlayers.name: Sadio Mané
keyPlayers.note: All-time top scorer (55) and the attack's reference point; clutch from the spot — 16 of 19 penalties, all of the last nine

keyPlayers.name: Iliman Ndiaye
keyPlayers.note: Outstanding dribbler and creator from the right

keyPlayers.name: Pape Gueye
keyPlayers.note: The secret weapon — long-range left-foot shooting (the AFCON match-winner vs Morocco)

tactics: Press on the front foot, control the ball, and slip runners in behind via early half-space balls; back themselves to come from behind when needed. The vulnerability is overplaying out of their own half against an organised press.
notes: Three of the last four AFCON finals; chasing their best World Cup run since the 2002 quarter-finals.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group I (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
